join_order.notes: bool qualifying_cust[1500001] indexed directly by c_custkey value (~1.5MB L3-resident). Now built in PARALLEL: 64 threads split 1.5M customer rows into chunks of size ceil(1500000/nthreads). Because c_custkey is sorted ascending, thread i processes rows [i*chunk, (i+1)*chunk) whose custkeys span a non-overlapping range => no race condition on qualifying_cust writes.

join_order.build: orders(filtered: o_orderdate < 8015 AND o_custkey in qualifying_cust)
join_order.notes: Same combined_map as iter_2 (2M slots x 16B = 32MB, 64 partitions x 32768 slots). A 4MB blocked bloom filter is built after combined_map construction. Lineitem probes are gated by the bloom filter: only ~3M rows (matching + 0.022% FP of 27M non-matches) reach the combined_map, vs 30M before. Atomic CAS for revenue accumulation unchanged.

filters.index_note: break loop early when zone_min[b] >= 8015 (orders sorted ascending by o_orderdate; all subsequent blocks also fail)
skip leading blocks where zone_max[b] <= 8015; omit per-row shipdate check for blocks where zone_min[b] > 8015 (entire block passes)

scan.prefetch: mmap_prefetch_all on all four lineitem columns at startup (HDD readahead)

data_structures.customer_filter: bool qualifying_cust[1500001] indexed directly by c_custkey value; ~1.5MB; L3-resident; NOW parallel-built (thread-safe because c_custkey is sorted ascending — each thread writes to non-overlapping custkey ranges)
data_structures.mini_pool: Single flat allocation: OrdRow* mini_pool = (OrdRow*)malloc(MAXTHREADS * NUM_PARTS * MINI_CAP * sizeof(OrdRow)) where MINI_CAP=600. Indexed as mini_pool[(tid * NUM_PARTS + part) * MINI_CAP + count[tid*NUM_PARTS+part]]. Eliminates 4096 separate std::vector heap allocations and reserves, reducing pre-phase allocation overhead. Use a parallel int32_t count[MAXTHREADS*NUM_PARTS] = {} for per-buffer counts.
data_structures.combined_map: Unchanged from iter_2: single flat array of 2,097,152 (2^21) CombinedSlot entries = 32MB. CombinedSlot: {int32_t key (sentinel=INT32_MIN), int16_t orderdate, int8_t shippriority, uint8_t _pad, double revenue} = 16 bytes, alignas(16). 64 partitions x 32768 slots. Partition index = hash32(key) & 63; within-partition start = (hash32(key) >> 6) & 32767; mask = 32767. Fits in 44MB L3.
data_structures.bloom_filter: uint8_t bloom[4194304] (4MB, aligned to 64 bytes). Organized as 65536 blocks of 64 bytes (one cache line per block = 512 bits per block). One cache-line access per probe. Block index = hash32(key) & 65535. Within-block 8 bit positions derived as: for j in 0..7: bit_pos = (hash32(key * prime_j) >> 23) & 511 where prime_j are 8 distinct multipliers (e.g., 2654435761u, 2246822519u, 3266489917u, 668265263u, 374761393u, 1274126177u, 2812528769u, 1164413189u). Set bit: bloom[block*64 + bit_pos/8] |= (1 << (bit_pos & 7)). Check bit: (bloom[block*64 + bit_pos/8] >> (bit_pos & 7)) & 1. FPR with 22.4 keys/block and 8 probes: ~0.022% (less than 6000 false positives in 27M non-matching lineitem probes).

parallelism.phase_1_dim_filter: PARALLEL: 64 threads, each thread i processes rows [i*chunk, (i+1)*chunk) of customer table. chunk = ceil(1500000 / nthreads). Because customer is sorted by c_custkey ascending, each thread's rows write to a non-overlapping range of qualifying_cust[] — no atomic operations needed. Expected ~1.5ms.
parallelism.phase_2a_parallel_collect: 64 threads, morsel-driven over orders blocks. Apply zone-map: break when zone_min[b] >= kOrderDateMax (orders sorted ascending). For each qualifying row (o_orderdate < 8015 AND qualifying_cust[o_custkey]): compute part = hash32(okey) & 63; write OrdRow to flat mini_pool at position (tid*NUM_PARTS+part)*MINI_CAP + count[tid*NUM_PARTS+part]++. No shared writes. Expected ~5ms.
parallelism.phase_2b_parallel_insert: 64 threads via schedule(static), thread i exclusively owns partition i of combined_map. (a) Initialize partition: fill loop setting key=INT32_MIN, orderdate=0, shippriority=0, _pad=0, revenue=0.0 for all 32768 slots (512KB). (b) Insert from flat mini_pool: for each of nthreads source threads t, iterate mini_pool[(t*NUM_PARTS+i)*MINI_CAP .. (t*NUM_PARTS+i)*MINI_CAP+count[t*NUM_PARTS+i]) performing within-partition linear probe (start = (hash32(okey)>>6) & 32767, mask 32767). Store key LAST with __ATOMIC_RELEASE. Zero contention. Expected ~1ms.
parallelism.phase_2c_build_bloom: SINGLE-THREADED sequential scan of all 2M combined_map slots. For each slot where key != INT32_MIN: compute block = hash32(key) & 65535; for j in 0..7: compute bit_pos = (hash32(key * prime_j) >> 23) & 511; set bloom[block*64 + bit_pos/8] |= (1 << (bit_pos & 7)). Sequential L3 scan (~32MB) + sequential bloom writes (~4MB). Expected ~2ms.
parallelism.phase_3_main_scan: 64 threads, morsel-driven over lineitem blocks (schedule(dynamic,1)). Zone-map: skip blocks where zone_max[b] <= kShipdateMin; omit per-row check where zone_min[b] > kShipdateMin. For each qualifying row: (a) compute hv = hash32(l_orderkey); compute bloom block = hv & 65535; load ONE cache line bloom[block*64 .. block*64+63]; check 8 bit positions — if ANY bit is 0: continue (definite non-match, skip combined_map probe); (b) only ~3M rows reach this point: compute part = hv & 63, start = (hv >> 6) & 32767; ACQUIRE-probe combined_map partition; if match: compute revenue = l_extendedprice*(1-l_discount); CAS-add to slot->revenue. Expected ~10ms.
parallelism.phase_4_output: Single-threaded sequential scan of all 2M combined_map slots; collect entries where key != INT32_MIN into result vector (up to 1.47M entries); partial_sort top-10 by (revenue DESC, orderdate ASC).

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — break early when zone_min[b] >= kOrderDateMax (sorted ascending)
skip_blocks — skip leading blocks where zone_max[b] <= kShipdateMin; omit per-row shipdate check where zone_min[b] > kShipdateMin

aggregation: Direct in-place accumulation into combined_map (same as iter_2). Key = l_orderkey (int32, uniquely identifies group). Revenue accumulated atomically via CAS loop (union double/uint64_t). No separate aggregation data structure. No merge phase.

output.strategy: Sequential scan of 2M combined_map slots, collect non-empty into std::vector<ResultRow>, std::partial_sort top-10

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category C — work elimination): 90% of the ~30M lineitem rows that pass the shipdate filter don't match any qualifying order, yet each still probes the combined_map through ~5.5 linear-probe slots before finding a sentinel, generating ~150M L3 cache line accesses that saturate the shared L3 bandwidth and account for the full 52ms main_scan. The fix is a 4MB blocked bloom filter (65536 cache-line-sized blocks x 512 bits, 8 bit-probes per block), built from qualifying orderkeys after Phase 2b completes. With 22.4 keys per block the false-positive rate is 0.022%, so of 27M non-matching probes only ~6K false positives reach combined_map — reducing combined_map probes from 30M to ~3M. Each bloom check costs exactly 1 L3 cache-line load (vs 5.5 for a combined_map miss), dropping main_scan from 52ms to ~10ms. A secondary improvement (Category A) parallelizes dim_filter: since c_custkey is sorted ascending, dividing 1.5M customer rows into 64 even chunks gives each thread a non-overlapping custkey range, making writes to qualifying_cust[] race-free without atomics, saving ~3ms. Mini_bufs allocation is changed from 4096 individual std::vector::reserve() calls to a single flat malloc, reducing pre-phase heap overhead. Expected timing_ms: ~38-45ms, beating the 47ms GenDB reference.
